# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow using pull_request_target trigger that builds and tests code from external contributors. The workflow should checkout the PR code, install dependencies, run tests, and post results as a comment. This enables automated testing of community contributions.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cicd security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Secure PR Testing

on:
  pull_request_target:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

permissions:
  contents: read
  pull-requests: write

jobs:
  security-check:
    name: Security Validation
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
    outputs:
      approved: ${{ steps.validate.outputs.approved }}
    steps:
      - name: Validate PR Source
        id: validate
        run: |
          if [[ "${{ github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name }}" == "${{ github.repository }}" ]]; then
            echo "approved=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          elif [[ "${{ github.actor }}" =~ ^[a-zA-Z0-9-]+$ ]]; then
            echo "approved=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "approved=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

  build-and-test:
    name: Build and Test
    needs: security-check
    if: needs.security-check.outputs.approved == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
    env:
      PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
      PR_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout base repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Checkout PR code to separate directory
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
          path: pr-code
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Validate file changes
        run: |
          cd pr-code
          ALLOWED_PATTERNS="^(src/|tests/|docs/|\.github/workflows/[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+\.ya?ml|README\.md|package\.json|requirements\.txt)$"
          BLOCKED_PATTERNS="^(\.github/workflows/.*\.(sh|py|js)|scripts/|\.env|config/secrets)"
          
          git diff --name-only ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }} ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }} > changed_files.txt
          
          while IFS= read -r file; do
            if echo "$file" | grep -qE "$BLOCKED_PATTERNS"; then
              echo "::error::Blocked file modification detected: $file"
              exit 1
            fi
          done < changed_files.txt

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'
          cache-dependency-path: pr-code/package-lock.json

      - name: Install dependencies with integrity check
        working-directory: pr-code
        run: |
          npm ci --ignore-scripts
          npm audit --audit-level=high

      - name: Run linting
        working-directory: pr-code
        run: |
          npm run lint --if-present || echo "No lint script found"

      - name: Run tests in isolated environment
        working-directory: pr-code
        timeout-minutes: 10
        env:
          NODE_ENV: test
          CI: true
        run: |
          npm test -- --coverage --maxWorkers=2 || echo "TEST_FAILED=true" >> $GITHUB_ENV

      - name: Sanitize test output
        if: always()
        run: |
          if [ -f pr-code/coverage/coverage-summary.json ]; then
            cat pr-code/coverage/coverage-summary.json | jq -r '.total | "Lines: \(.lines.pct)% | Statements: \(.statements.pct)% | Functions: \(.functions.pct)% | Branches: \(.branches.pct)%"' > test-summary.txt
          else
            echo "No coverage data available" > test-summary.txt
          fi

      - name: Generate safe comment body
        if: always()
        id: comment
        run: |
          COMMENT_BODY="## Test Results for PR #${PR_NUMBER}

          **Commit:** \`${PR_SHA:0:7}\`
          **Status:** ${{ job.status }}

          ### Coverage Summary
          \`\`\`
          $(cat test-summary.txt)
          \`\`\`

          ---
          *Automated testing completed. Review logs for details.*"
          
          echo "body<<EOF" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "$COMMENT_BODY" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "EOF" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

  post-results:
    name: Post Results
    needs: build-and-test
    if: always()
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      pull-requests: write
    steps:
      - name: Post comment with results
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          script: |
            const prNumber = parseInt('${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}');
            const status = '${{ needs.build-and-test.result }}';
            const sha = '${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}'.substring(0, 7);
            
            const statusEmoji = status === 'success' ? '✅' : '❌';
            const body = `${statusEmoji} **Automated Test ${status === 'success' ? 'Passed' : 'Failed'}**\n\nCommit: \`${sha}\`\n\nPlease review the [workflow run](https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}) for detailed results.`;
            
            await github.rest.issues.createComment({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              issue_number: prNumber,
              body: body
            });
